summary.intent: Address a set of Codex review findings on PR #710 (ars-ui repo) covering controlled selection state, GridList prop sync, non-composite shortcut dispatch, spec snippet correctness, and a wasm fake timer compile issue.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were patched (prop sync clears stale action requests, non-composite shortcuts dispatch correctly, select spec uses Duration correctly, fake interval delay shadowing fixed), regression tests added, all local checks passed (fmt, clippy, spec validate, coverage, wasm check), and changes committed and pushed. The controlled-selection thread was resolved and a fresh Codex review triggered.

repo: GridList has a tight branch coverage threshold — any behavioral change to GridList requires re-running cargo xcov to avoid threshold failures.
The project uses cargo xtask for CI tasks: `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets`, `cargo xtask spec validate`, and `cargo xtask ci clippy` are the canonical verification commands.
Spec snippets in spec/components/selection/select.md do not import Duration, so fully qualified `core::time::Duration::ZERO` must be used in those examples.
The wasm target (wasm32-unknown-unknown) must be explicitly checked for ars-test-harness since fake timer hooks can introduce JsValue/Duration shadowing issues that only surface there.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and its status is tracked via reactions (e.g., 'eyes') and unresolved review threads via GraphQL.

code.finding: Prop sync path did not clear stale requested_action_key; fixed so set_props resets it.
Non-composite keydown handler was swallowing non-navigation shortcuts; patched to dispatch ClearSelection/SelectAll correctly.
Fake interval hook had a JsValue/Duration variable shadow on the delay parameter — a P1 wasm compile issue.
Spec snippets referenced Duration without import; corrected to use core::time::Duration::ZERO fully qualified.

workflow: GraphQL is used directly via `gh api graphql` to query unresolved Codex review threads, which is more reliable than polling PR comments.

CI matrix jobs are staggered: formatting and snapshot checks finish first, then Clippy, then unit/adapter/i18n, then coverage/release/feature-flag groups — useful for knowing when to give up waiting and address new review feedback.
Resolving GitHub review threads requires the GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation with the thread node ID, not a REST call.
cargo xcov is the instrumented coverage runner; it must be awaited for the threshold summary before considering coverage passing.

friction: CI matrix has many long-running jobs (coverage, release verification, Dioxus feature flags) requiring multiple multi-minute polling intervals before knowing final status.
Codex posted four new findings only after all but the final three CI checks had passed, meaning a full re-wait cycle was needed after the new commit.
The controlled-selection thread resolution required both a REST reply and a separate GraphQL mutation — two separate API calls with no combined endpoint.
Repeated sleep/poll cycles with write_stdin keepalives are awkward; the transcript shows ~6 polling rounds before Codex findings appeared.
open_items: Codex review pass was re-triggered at end of session but results were not yet available — final Codex approval is still pending.
Coverage threshold for GridList is described as 'tight'; the threshold itself should be reviewed or relaxed if behavioral changes keep requiring full re-runs.
Selection interactions can still compound from pending requested keys (noted in the controlled-selection thread reply) — this is a known limitation left for a follow-up.
